title: Aksa Beach
tagline: Aksa Beach — beach town of Maharashtra

overview.short: Aksa Beach is a popular beach and vacation spot in Aksa village, Malad, Mumbai, India.
overview.description: Aksa Beach is a popular beach and vacation spot in Aksa village, Malad, Mumbai, India. It is situated close to Marvé Beach. It is a popular weekend destination, dotted with many private cottages and hotels, some of which are rented out to tourists and visitors. Aksa Beach also happens to be one of the cleanest beaches in the city of Mumbai.

nearestAirport.name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Manori Creek
topPlaces.description: Manori Creek (also Malad Creek) is a creek (tidal channel) on Salsette Island, northern Mumbai (Bombay), India. It is also known as Gorai Creek further north.

topPlaces.name: Marve Island
topPlaces.description: Marve Island was among a group of islands that lay to the west of Salsette island near Mumbai, India. Manori and Gorai on Dharavi Island lay to its north while Erangal and Madh on Madh Island lay to its south.

topPlaces.name: Marvé Beach
topPlaces.description: Marve Beach (Pronunciation: [maːɾʋe]) is located in the western suburb of Malad in the city of Mumbai, India. Marathi is the most spoken language in this region.

topPlaces.name: Oshiwara River
topPlaces.description: Oshiwara River is a river in Mumbai, India. It begins in the Aarey Milk Colony, cuts through the Goregaon hills, across the Aarey Milk Colony before emptying into the Malad Creek.

topPlaces.name: Manori
topPlaces.description: Manori is a village located on Dharavi Bet in northern Mumbai, India. It is known for its beach along India's west coast and the Manori Creek.

topPlaces.name: Poisar River
topPlaces.description: Poisar River is a river in Mumbai, India. It begins in the Sanjay Gandhi National Park and empties into the Marve Creek and finally into the Arabian Sea.

topPlaces.name: Bangur Nagar
topPlaces.description: Bangur Nagar is a residential area in Goregaon West, Mumbai, India. It was developed by the Bangur Group of Kolkata in the mid-1970s.

topPlaces.name: Malad West metro station
topPlaces.description: Malad West (officially known as Motilal Oswal - Malad West) is an elevated metro station serving the Ekta Nagar neighbourhood of Malad, on the North-South corridor of the Yellow Line 2A of the Mumbai Metro in Mumbai, India. This station is owned by the Mumbai Metropolitan Region Development Authority (MMRDA), and was inaugurated on 19 January 2023.

items.note: Manori Creek (also Malad Creek) is a creek (tidal channel) on Salsette Island, northern Mumbai (Bombay), India. It is also known as Gorai Creek further north.

Marve Island was among a group of islands that lay to the west of Salsette island near Mumbai, India. Manori and Gorai on Dharavi Island lay to its north while Erangal and Madh on Madh Island lay to its south.
Marve Beach (Pronunciation: [maːɾʋe]) is located in the western suburb of Malad in the city of Mumbai, India. Marathi is the most spoken language in this region.
Oshiwara River is a river in Mumbai, India. It begins in the Aarey Milk Colony, cuts through the Goregaon hills, across the Aarey Milk Colony before emptying into the Malad Creek.
Manori is a village located on Dharavi Bet in northern Mumbai, India. It is known for its beach along India's west coast and the Manori Creek.
Poisar River is a river in Mumbai, India. It begins in the Sanjay Gandhi National Park and empties into the Marve Creek and finally into the Arabian Sea.
Bangur Nagar is a residential area in Goregaon West, Mumbai, India. It was developed by the Bangur Group of Kolkata in the mid-1970s.

Malad West (officially known as Motilal Oswal - Malad West) is an elevated metro station serving the Ekta Nagar neighbourhood of Malad, on the North-South corridor of the Yellow Line 2A of the Mumbai Metro in Mumbai, India. This station is owned by the Mumbai Metropolitan Region Development Authority (MMRDA), and was inaugurated on 19 January 2023.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Aksa Beach?
faq.a: The best time to visit Aksa Beach is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Aksa Beach?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (12 km) and the nearest railway station is Mumbai CSMT (27 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Aksa Beach?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Aksa Beach at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Aksa Beach?
faq.a: Stays in Aksa Beach start from ₹1,200 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Aksa Beach?
faq.a: The top attractions are Manori Creek, Marve Island, Marvé Beach, Oshiwara River, Manori.
